compiled_state.title: red-zone.sh parse_failing_tests_from_output misses indented FAIL lines
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: parse_failing_tests_from_output in plugins/dso/hooks/lib/red-zone.sh uses ^FAIL: (line start anchor) to extract failing test names. Most test files in tests/scripts/ use 2-space indented FAIL output (echo "  FAIL: test_name"), making them invisible to the parser. When no test names are parsed, the fail-safe treats ALL failures as blocking, defeating RED zone tolerance. Fix: support indented FAIL lines (e.g., grep -oE '(^|\s)FAIL: [a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*').
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 1 (BASIC). Single function parse_failing_tests_from_output in red-zone.sh line 85 uses ^FAIL: anchor, missing 203 indented FAIL: lines across 20 test files.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
